steps.title: Find the subject
steps.content: Strip the parenthetical: "behavioral economists such as Gee, whose research focuses on charitable giving, ___ that..." The subject of the verb is behavioral economists — plural.

steps.title: Match subject-verb agreement
steps.content:
Identify the grammar issue: subject-verb agreement. Rule: a plural subject takes a plural present-tense verb (base form).
  • Choice A — "has contended": singular present-perfect verb; disagrees with plural "behavioral economists" and shifts to an unnecessary perfect tense. ✗
  • Choice B — "contends": singular present-tense verb; disagrees with plural subject. ✗
  • Choice C — "is contending": singular present-progressive verb; disagrees with plural subject. ✗
  • Choice D — "contend": base-form (plural) present-tense verb; agrees with plural "behavioral economists." ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
